name: Tax disputes
window_name: Tax dispute resolution — lawyers by MRZ LEGAL.

blocks.text_main:
The settlement of tax disputes typically relates to assessing the validity of a tax benefit, particularly with respect to the calculation and payment of value added or company income taxes.

These main sources of public revenue take large assets out of the company’s turnover. However, the participant’s ability to fulfill their duty to pay taxes relies on generating sales and profits from economic activities.

Entrepreneurship frequently consists of various transactions that tend to involve internal tax optimisation, and the tax authority may qualify them in accordance with their true economic purpose, determining the real size of the taxpayer’s obligation.

As organisations expand in size and the value generated becomes greater, they increasingly come under the watch of authorities, because high incomes push the beneficiaries to avoid paying taxes by seeking unjustified tax benefits in their favour.

The risks of a tax benefit being recognised as unjustified exist not only when participating in unjustified tax evasion transactions or working with the main purpose of obtaining it but also when failing to exercise due diligence in choosing a counterparty.

As a result, if any funds are not received in the budget, they are written off from accounts based on the decision made by a tax authority. Subsequently, during a tax dispute, courts evaluate the legality of this action, particularly if the taxpayer’s assets have been lost, leading to a cessation of their activities.

Our ability lies in pinpointing the appropriate reasoning for each dispute and expertly showcasing the validity of tax optimization methods and logical business approaches.
